entry.title: DI API Disconnecting with Error: You are not connected to a company
entry.content:
I Have a DI API Windwos Service that works fine for 3-5 days with no errors, and then i get the You are not connected to a company, error.

I haven't  found i solution for this.

I am Using .NET 10 with various various threads using a singleton DI API  Company object Instance, i don't know if calling the GetBusinessObject method is not thread safe, the same with Recordset.DoQuery and adding or update any objects or services.

here is a snippet of the singleton

    internal sealed class DIAPI : IDIAPI
    {
        public Company Company { get; private set; }

        private readonly string _path;
        private readonly ILogger<DIAPI> _logger;

        public DIAPI(ILogger<DIAPI> logger, string path)
        {
            _logger = logger;
            _path = path;
            Company = new Company();
            (bool conected, string error) = Connect(_path);
            if (!conected)
            {
                _logger.LogError("erro ao tentar conectar: {erro}", error);
            }
        }

        private (bool connected , string error) Connect(string path)
        {
            if (Company is not null && Company.Connected)
                return (true, "");

            // parametros de conexão
            Conf conf = Conf.GetConfiguration(path);
            if(conf.DBType is null)
                throw new Exception("Tipo de banco de dados não informado na configuração.");

            string erro = string.Empty;
            Company = new()
            {
                LicenseServer = conf.LicenceServer,
                CompanyDB = conf.CompanyDB,
                UserName = conf.UserName,
                Password = conf.Password,
                DbServerType = (BoDataServerTypes)conf.DBType, // SQL 2019 é 15
                SLDServer = conf.SLDServer,
                Server = conf.Server,
                DbUserName = conf.UserDB,
                DbPassword = conf.PassDB,
                language = BoSuppLangs.ln_Portuguese_Br,
                UseTrusted = false
            };

            if (Company.Connect() != 0)
            {
                erro = $"{Company.GetLastErrorCode()} | {Company.GetLastErrorDescription()}";
                return (false, erro);
            }

            return (true, "");
        }
}

here is an exeple of injecting the singleton in a class

public Email_Queue (ILogger<Email_Queue> logger, IDIAPI api) { _api = api; _logger = logger; }


and using like this for exemple inside the many threads.

 

Recordset recordset = _api.Company.GetBusinessObject(BoObjectTypes.BoRecordset);


 Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Rewards and Recognition - Certificate is not loading
entry.content:
@swagatikapanda Can you check Provisioning and get the logs for this failed job ? you can follow the same process than I show on this blog.

 

Also did you follow the following steps ?

  1. duplicate your current certificate
  2. strip the duplicated version of anything complex
  3. See if it loads this time
  4. if it does then rebuild it slowly, item by item, until you find what is creating the issues in loading.

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition - Job Scheduler failing while running the job for Milestone Award
entry.content:
I'm looking at it with product team and in jiras. 
Can you share the details of your milestone award and also can you share the full logs from provisioning?

 

 

Additionally please review the below from Claude: 

 

I couldn't find a public SAP KBA matching that exact stack trace (sqlToGetUserId is null in SQLWithBindVars.getSql()), so this looks like it hasn't been documented publicly yet — but the error itself is diagnosable from how the "Identifies eligible users for milestone awards" job works internally.

What's happening

That job builds a dynamic SQL query to pull the list of eligible userIds based on how you've configured Eligibility on the Milestone Program (either "Eligibility Rules" using an EC rule on the spotAwardEligibility base object, or "Dynamic Groups"). The SQLWithBindVars object is supposed to be populated with that generated SQL and then executed — the NPE means the job never got that far: it tried to call .getSql() on an object that was never constructed. That points to the eligibility configuration itself failing to resolve into a valid query, not a data/runtime issue with a specific user.

Where to look, in order of likelihood

  1. Dynamic Groups path (most common cause of this specific NPE):
    • Open the Milestone Program → Eligibility settings and confirm which mode is selected.
    • If Dynamic Groups: go to Admin Center → Manage Data → find the Dynamic Group object tied to this program and check it actually has criteria rows defined. An empty/unpublished Dynamic Group, or one whose base object criteria reference a field that's since been removed/renamed in the corporate data model, will fail to generate SQL — that's exactly the null-SQL symptom.
    • Check the group hasn't been deleted or is in a draft/unpublished state while still being referenced by the program.
  2. Eligibility Rules path:
    • If using EC rules on spotAwardEligibility, open Configure Business Rules and validate the rule still compiles cleanly — a rule referencing a deleted/renamed MDF field or foundation object will silently fail to generate a base query rather than throwing a rule-validation error at save time.
  3. Re-save to force regeneration: Sometimes the cached rule/group reference is stale even if the config looks fine in the UI. Go into the program's Eligibility step, re-select the same Dynamic Group or Rule, and Save again — this forces the program to re-persist the association and can clear a broken cached reference.
  4. Check for orphaned/legacy config: If this program was cloned or migrated from another one, confirm the eligibility object wasn't left pointing at a group/rule ID that belongs to a different program type (Spot Award vs Milestone use different base object types even though they share the spotAwardEligibility rule type).

If none of that resolves it

Since this is an InternalServerErrorException (category InternalError) — not a validation error — with a raw NPE in server-side RBP DAO code, there's a real chance the eligibility config is in a state the UI allowed but the backend can't handle (a known class of bug in Milestone Awards, which is still a relatively new module). If steps 1–3 don't clear it, this is worth an incident to SAP Product Support with the exact stack trace and component LOD-SF-RNR — they can check server logs for which program/group ID it's failing against, which you can't see from the client side.

@manish_rustagi462677

entry.title: Re: Job Approval in SmartRecruiters: Overview, Features, and Configuration Guide
entry.content:
Hi @SimonKjge ,

You cannot directly create custom questions or attachment fields in SmartRecruiters. However, you can create custom fields on the requisition form to capture the required information. For attachments, you can use the standard Attachments section available on the requisition form. As of now, SmartRecruiters does not support creating custom attachment fields. Therefore, any documents that need to be uploaded by the hiring leader must be captured using the standard attachment field provided by the system.

Thanks,

Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: Qeries related to Power BI integration with S/4hana
entry.content:
Hi there,

Yes, Power BI can be integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Direct access through the native SAP HANA connector is one option, but it requires database connectivity, the HANA client, network configuration, and appropriate database permissions.

You may also evaluate Power BI Connector for SAP, available on SAP Store:

https://www.sap.com/products/data-cloud/partners/metrica-software-inc-power-bi-connector-for-sap.html

It connects through SAP BTP and activated S/4HANA OData services without requiring direct HANA database access. Users can select services, entities, fields, parameters, and server-side filters, then connect Power BI to the generated OData endpoint.

This workflow supports scheduled and incremental refresh. It depends on the OData services available in your SAP environment and is not equivalent to HANA SQL or native HANA DirectQuery.

The connector requires a separate subscription. SAP and BTP licensing requirements should be confirmed with your SAP account manager.

A 30-day free trial is available.

Documentation:
https://metricasoftware.com/docs/sap/

Cheers,

Anton

entry.title: Re: Auto Salary Calculation Not Available in SmartRecruit...
entry.content: As you are aware, this cannot be achieved directly in SmartRecruiters. However, it can be implemented through a custom integration. One possible approach is to use middleware such as SAP Cloud Integration (CPI). You can create a custom MDF object in SuccessFactors to store all the required compensation fields, along with the criteria used for the calculations, such as grade, location, and any other relevant attributes required by SmartRecruiters. The business rules for the compensation calculations can be configured within the custom MDF object. Once the rules are in place, the required data (such as Offered CTC, Grade, Location, etc.) can be sent from SmartRecruiters to the custom MDF object through the integration. Based on the configured business rules, the remaining compensation components will be calculated automatically. After the calculations are completed, the calculated compensation details can be sent back from the custom MDF object to SmartRecruiters, where they can be displayed or used in the recruitment process.

entry.title: Re: SmartRecruiters to SAP SuccessFactors EC Integration...
entry.content:
To map fields between SmartRecruiters and SuccessFactors, you can use the Integration Hub available in SmartRecruiters. However, not all fields are currently supported for mapping. For example, pay components and certain country-specific fields are not supported through the standard Integration Hub. For fields that are not supported, you will need to build a custom integration using SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) or any other middleware used in your landscape. This custom integration can be used to transfer the unsupported fields between SmartRecruiters and SuccessFactors.

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: Re: SmartRecruiters - Assign Hiring Team Roles in Bulk /...
entry.content:
Hi @Arisp1 ,

If you are using the Hiring Team, users must be added individually, as SmartRecruiters does not currently provide an option to add multiple users at once. Alternatively, you can create an Access Group based on your business requirements. Users who are members of the Access Group will automatically receive access to all requisitions and applications that match the criteria defined for that group. Although the Access Group itself is not visible on the requisition form, it can effectively meet your client's requirement by automatically granting access to requisitions and applications based on the configured criteria, eliminating the need to add users manually to each requisition.

Thanks,
Sanjeet

entry.title: August 2026 – Cloud ERP Expert-Guided Implementation (EGI) Schedule
entry.content:
Join us for our upcoming live sessions this August! Explore the Expert-guided Implementation (EGI) schedule below and register today to secure your spot.

Expert-guided Implementations (EGIs) are multi-day, remote, workshop-style sessions where participants work directly in their own system landscapes to learn how to customize and execute complex activities. Join experienced SAP service engineers for hands-on workshops focused on Cloud ERP. It's a great opportunity to learn from experts, ask questions, and gain practical skills that can help keep your implementation on track.

Activating Joule: The Pre-Requisites

Description: In this EGI session, we'll walk you through the key prerequisites and technical setup needed to activate Joule, while helping you understand its capabilities, architecture, and deployment.

For details, please read the abstract here

EMEA Region: August 10-11, 2026 | Register


Unlocking AI: Joule and SAP Business AI from Strategy to Activation

Description: In this session, you'll work alongside SAP experts in a hands-on workshop to explore Joule and discover how Business AI can enhance processes and improve productivity across your SAP landscape.

For details, please read the abstract here

EMEA Region: August 10-12, 2026 | Register
APJ Region: August 17-19, 2026 | Register
NA Region: August 24-26, 2026 | Register
LA Region (Spanish): August 31 – September 2, 2026 | Register

📆  Visit the Cloud ERP (Public) value map events calendar entry linked above to register. To access the Value Map Groups, login to the SAP Community using an S-user ID or a Universal ID.

📝  Note: Access to EGI sessions require a one-time registration to SAP Learning.


Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps
Beyond Expert-guided Implementation sessions, SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. Value maps on SAP Community bring together guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next - all included in your SAP cloud subscription.

👉  Watch our short demo video, then head over to our page to explore our range of topics and request a call with a topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals.

About the author:
Kristen Reddington is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in SAP Cloud ERP solutions. Kristen brings a diverse background centered on customer engagement and success. In her current role, she focuses on guiding organizations through their Cloud ERP transformation journeys, enabling them to accelerate the adoption of SAP Cloud ERP solutions through tailored value map content and SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

entry.title: Re: Calling offline Cap Service, throwing error on passin...
entry.content:
I don't believe Offline supports storing Action Import calls.  I believe Function Imports can be stored offline but I have not looked into either lately.  

If you are using offline I would suggest just using the standard OData CRUD operations and adjust your service if necessary.

entry.title: Re: error on calling a odata service from sap mdk app
entry.content:
I think your action override is incorrect.  I believe the individual parameters for the Call Function are under a Parameters property in the structure of the action.  So in your executeAction call with the overrides you need to wrap all your properties with the Parameters block.

await context.executeAction(
   {
      "Name": "/SyncApp/Actions/Docket/CreateDeliveryNote_G/CreateDeliveryNote_Online.action",
      "Properties": 
      {
         "Parameters": {
            // "Parameters": payload
            "contractNo": payload.contractNo,
            "custPoNumber": payload.custPoNumber,
            "deliveryDate": payload.deliveryDate,
            "deliveryTime": payload.deliveryTime,
            "locationText": payload.locationText,
            "notes": payload.notes,
            "plantId": payload.plantId,
            "plantName": payload.plantName,
            "products": payload.products,
            "services": payload.services,
            "shipToId": payload.shipToId,
            "shipToName1": payload.shipToName1,
            "shipToName2": payload.shipToName2,
            "soldToId": payload.soldToId,
            "soldToName1": payload.soldToName1,
            "soldToName2": payload.soldToName2,
            "shotId": payload.shotId,
            "vehicleOperators": payload.vehicleOperators
         }
      }
   }
);

entry.title: SAP Developer News, July 23rd, 2026
entry.content:
DESCRIPTION

ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code Version 1.1

  • Blog Post: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/abap-development-tools-for-visual-studio-code-version-1-1-released/ba-p/14421143

What’s New in SAP Build: Q1 & Q2 2026 Release Highlights

  • SAP Build Release Highlights Blog post: https://community.sap.com/t5/tooling-sap-build-blog-posts/what-s-new-in-sap-build-q1-amp-q2-2026-release-highlights/ba-p/14444726
  • Developer News on CAP AI Plugin: https://community.sap.com/t5/developer-news/sap-developer-news-july-9th-2026/ba-p/14436806
  • Developer News on Change Tracking 2.0 Plugin: https://community.sap.com/t5/developer-news/sap-developer-news-for-april-30th-2026/ba-p/14387145
  • Learning Journey on Introduction to Joule Studio: https://learning.sap.com/courses/introducing-joule-studio

SAP Business Client 8.10 Joule for SAPGUI

  • SAP Business Client Details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-business-client-8-10/ba-p/14429327
  • SAPGUI Details: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-gui-for-windows-8-10-is-coming-general-availability-as-of-16th-of-july/ba-p/14434135

SAP Business AI: Release 2026-Q2

  • Release Highlights Q2 2026: https://news.sap.com/2026/07/sap-business-ai-release-highlights-q2-2026/

SAP Connect Day for UX in Newtown Square September 2nd

  • Event Page: https://events.sap.com/noam-connect-day-for-ux-newtown-square-pa/en_us/home.html

CHAPTER TITLES

0:00 Intro

0:10 ABAP Development Tools for Visual Studio Code Version 1.1

1:06 What’s New in SAP Build: Q1 & Q2 2026 Release Highlights

5:53 SAP Business Client 8.10 Joule for SAPGUI

7:10 SAP Business AI: Release Highlights Q2

10:01 Connect Day for UX in Newtown Square Sept 2

Transcription

00;00;01;19 - 00;00;09;10
Announcer
This is the SAP Developer News for July the 23rd, 2026.

00;00;09;12 - 00;00;09;23
Mamikee Kanneh
ABAP

00;00;09;26 - 00;00;43;28
Mamikee Kanneh
Developing Tools for Visual Studio Code Version 1.1 is now available. Bring in more development capabilities and usability improvement to the extension. This release adds support for several highly requested ABAP object types, including classic ABAP source code program, and include function groups and function modules, as well as additional dictionary and wrap objects. Version 1.1 also expands the available MCP tools, giving AI coding agents more ways to interact with ABAP systems and support development tasks directly within Visual Studio Code.

00;00;43;28 - 00;01;07;03
Mamikee Kanneh
And based on the feedback from the first release, we introduced an additional set of usability improvements to make everyday development workflow smoother. This is another major step towards expanding the ABAP development experience in Visual Studio Code. Now to explore the full list of new features and updates included in version 1.1. Head to the block post links in the description below.

00;01;07;05 - 00;01;44;03
Shrini Neelamegam
Hello everyone! Q1 and Q2 of 2026 brought massive updates across SAP Build covering Work Zone, CAP, SAP Process Automation, and the Joule Studio for development. Let's dive in. In SAP Build Work Zone. Customizing your experience is easier with Myspace. A single page to organize all your favorite control on the AI front. Next generation is now integrated into the Text widget toolbar, so you can draft or refine Copy on the fly.

00;01;44;06 - 00;02;19;11
Shrini Neelamegam
Plus, Joule Work Zone Advance can now create entire workspaces from scratch and quickly locate specific content on the mobile front. SAP Mobile Start is now rebranded as Mobile App. It features a refreshed look. Voice dictation for prompts and seamless single sign on for web apps in version 3.2. Version 3.1 brings dynamic spaces to group your key tools, pinning options, and custom admin support channels.

00;02;19;14 - 00;02;53;12
Shrini Neelamegam
Finally, MDK 26.6 introduces pre onboarding, compliance checks, stronger security, and embedded AI capabilities. Over in Cap, we have two major plugin updates. Antonio previously gave a deep dive into the cap AI plugin, and DJ Adams covered the Change Tracking 2.2 plugin in an earlier developer news episode. Check out their segments link below for the full backstory and the latest details for SAP UI5

00;02;53;14 - 00;03;34;12
Shrini Neelamegam
Keeping apps modern is much easier. The new UI modernization plugin automates legacy upgrades like replacing deprecated APIs, and works with Claude Code and Copilot as well. The updated unify MCP server adds three new tools to help LLMs build UI integration cards, following best practices for accessibility. Business users can already adapt theory apps at runtime using key user adaptation, but when business requirements get too complex for low code tools, you can now seamlessly bridge the gap to developer extensibility.

00;03;34;15 - 00;04;08;08
Shrini Neelamegam
When creating an adaptation project in SAP Business Application Studio, simply select the brand new Import Key User Changes option to pull those runtime edits straight into your DevOps space. And if you are building SAP unify apps with TypeScript. Be sure to check out our new developer guide detailing the major transitions happening across the TypeScript ecosystem. Next up, let's talk about SAP Build Process Automation.

00;04;08;11 - 00;04;40;21
Shrini Neelamegam
Monitoring your workflows gets a major upgrade with instance level attributes. You can now attach real business context directly to running processes, giving operational teams much clearer visibility into key details and helping them make faster, better informed decisions. To help you stay ahead of operational issues, you can now set up proactive email notifications for processing failures or skip instances.

00;04;40;23 - 00;05;15;07
Shrini Neelamegam
Plus, new environment filters make governance simpler by letting you view or restrict process visibility based on where your workflow slip, like dev, test or production. Handling errors is now much smoother thanks to new configurable retry mechanisms and structured exception handling right at the action level, so temporary hiccups don't derail your entire process. Finally, if you are looking to dive into Joule Studio, we have got you great news!

00;05;15;08 - 00;05;40;13
Shrini Neelamegam
The brand new free learning course Introducing Joule Studio is now live on SAP learning. This course gives you the complete big picture showing how Jules studio fits into the SAP business AI platform. What you can achieve with your and you'll work, and how to build agents and orchestrate end to end workflows with native.

00;05;40;15 - 00;05;53;21
Shrini Neelamegam
You can find the link to the full Release Highlights blog post, along with direct link to every feature and the learning journey we cover today down in the video description below. Thanks for watching.

00;05;53;23 - 00;06;20;15
Thomas Jung
Last week when many of the developer advocates were together for Code Connect. We were at dinner one evening and we were talking about possible demos for SAP TechEd and we kind of joked about building a Joule interface for the SAP GUI. And lo and behold, this week I woke up and read an article about the new version of the SAP Business Client.

00;06;20;16 - 00;06;45;05
Thomas Jung
The wraps around the SAP GUI, and sure enough, that's one of the major features that the brand new 8.1 version of the business brings to the SAP experience. The business client, formerly the NetWeaver Business Client, has long been a tool for wrapping around the SAP GUI and providing additional navigation and functionality. Used to be that you used it for the sidecar widgets.

00;06;45;08 - 00;07;11;17
Thomas Jung
Now it can be used to wrap the Joule interface and allow Joule interaction with SAP GUI transactions. So if that sounds like something you'd be interested in. Check out the links below in the show notes to download and set up the newest version of both SAP GUI and the SAP Business Client to try out that new feature.

00;07;11;19 - 00;07;45;13
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
In SAP Business AI platform 2026 Q2 focuses on three technical areas building AI solutions, improving model reasoning and observability, and governing agents at scale in the build layer. The new Joule 2D unifies design, extension and production of AI agents, apps and workflows. It generates requirements and technical specs from context. Applies a well based guardrails to AI coding assistance under SAP managed controls, and support third party or SAP models.

00;07;45;15 - 00;08;19;29
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
Also in build, the SAP S/4 HANA custom code migration agent now generally available, automates above migration from IC to S for it runs above test cockpit checks across custom code, interprets findings, categorizes issues and applies deterministic fixes plus AI based changes with confidence scores in contextualize intrusion. The generative AI hub adds a batch API for high volume foundational model workloads, with asynchronous job submission and results written to an object store.

00;08;20;02 - 00;08;52;05
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
The inference observability service centralizes logging of prompts, responses and context, supports response rating, and enables data set export for fine tuning and benchmarking. Speech to speech recognition is now available for end to end voice experiences as well as SAP document AI hot model selection for document processing. Three new standard document types and configurable document level confidence ranges shown in the document header.

00;08;52;08 - 00;09;27;04
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
SAP domain models trained on SAP domain knowledge help create clean, core compliant extensions in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP Ariba from natural language and query customer data grounded in data models and business contexts. This models run under the hood and are not directly exposed to customers and partners in the governance. The SAP AI Agent Hub provides a single control plane for AI agents, large language models, and MCP servers.

00;09;27;09 - 00;10;01;23
Vitaliy Rudnytskiy
It automates asset discovery across Microsoft, Google, AWS, ServiceNow, and SAP. AI core delivers structured governments governance assessment and uses a verification badge integrated with runtime solutions to control approved agents and MCP servers. Future plans cover runtime observability, identity and access control agent in protest mining and workforce impact mapping. Full release highlights for business IQ to 2026 are available via the link in the description.

00;10;01;26 - 00;10;29;00
Thomas Jung
I want to tell you about the upcoming Connect Day for User Experience. This is planned for September 2nd in the SAP Newtown Square offices. So if you're on the east coast of the US, this is an opportunity for you to come together with your fellow SAP developers and UX designers, as well as experts from SAP to discuss all things user experience.

00;10;29;02 - 00;10;50;11
Thomas Jung
There's so much happening in this area right now with the impact of AI on both the creation of user experience, as well as embedding AI into the user experience. It's going to be so much to talk about that day, and we wanted to tell you well in advance so you can mark your calendar. We'll remind you again on the developer news is we get closer to that date.

00;10;50;13 - 00;11;04;28
Thomas Jung
I plan to be there for that event. I'm coming into town, and I think Rich Holman from the Developer Advocates team will be there as well. So if you're interested in talking to us and others about user experience, this is going to be a great opportunity. We look forward to seeing you there.

entry.title: Somos SAP Community Curitiba – Marssel Vilaça
entry.content:
Este post faz parte da série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, criada para compartilhar as histórias das pessoas que ajudam a construir e fortalecer o ecossistema SAP na nossa cidade. Fui convidado por @SimoneAzevedo para participar desta série e compartilhar um pouco da minha trajetória na SAP Community Curitiba.

Quem sou eu na SAP Community Curitiba?

Sou Marssel Vilaça e acredito que comunidades existem para aproximar pessoas. Entrei na SAP Community em 2007 procurando respostas para dúvidas técnicas durante meus primeiros anos como consultor SAP. O que eu não imaginava era que aquele espaço mudaria impactaria tanto na minha carreira. Tenho um blog pessoal publicado aqui nesta série BIF.

Com o passar dos anos comecei a responder perguntas, escrever blogs, ministrar palestras, produzir vídeos, organizar eventos e incentivar outras pessoas a também compartilharem conhecimento.

Hoje atuo como SAP Champion, curador da SAP Community Recife, São Paulo e também  da SAP Community Curitiba desde suas primeiras iniciativas.

O que mais me motiva...

Compartilhar conhecimento multiplica oportunidades. Talvez o maior patrimônio da SAP Community nunca tenha sido a tecnologia. Sempre foram as pessoas.

Como fazer parte da comunidade impactou minha jornada profissional ou pessoal?

Grande parte das oportunidades profissionais que tive nasceu de pessoas que conheceram meu trabalho através da comunidade. Muitos clientes, projetos, palestras, treinamentos e amizades surgiram depois que comecei a compartilhar conhecimento de forma voluntária.

Momento especial

Sem dúvidas, foi muito especial poder contribuir para os primeiros passos da SAP Community Curitiba. Ver essa comunidade crescer, reunir pessoas tão engajadas e realizar eventos de alto nível mostra que, quando o trabalho é voluntário e feito com propósito, os resultados aparecem naturalmente.

Conecte-se comigo:

https://about.me/mabv

Convidados para continuar a série
Para continuar a série Somos SAP Community Curitiba, convido: @LuizSouza , @AdrianoCWB

Marssel Vilaça

entry.title: Re: June Developer Challenge - CAP Knowledge - Task 07 - Authorization annotations

entry.title: Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management - What's New in 2608
entry.content:
Hello SAP Supply Chain Management community!

As global supply chains continue to evolve in complexity and scale, organizations need solutions that can adapt quickly while maintaining operational excellence. The 2608 release cycle brings a comprehensive set of enhancements across the SAP Supply Chain Management portfolio, designed to address the real-world challenges you face every day, from improving planning accuracy and manufacturing efficiency to enhancing collaboration across your extended supply chain network.

This release demonstrates SAP's continued commitment to innovation across the entire supply chain portfolio. Whether you're looking to optimize your planning processes with SAP Integrated Business Planning, streamline manufacturing operations with SAP Digital Manufacturing, or enhance collaboration through SAP Business Network solutions, the 2608 release delivers meaningful capabilities that drive tangible business value.

In this post, we highlight what’s new across key products and, more importantly, point you to the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management, where you can explore detailed feature descriptions, walk-thru simulations, and enablement content to support your adoption journey. Let's explore what's new across each solution area.

 

  • What's New in Supply Chain Management
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP)
  • SAP Digital Manufacturing
  • SAP Business Network for Logistics
  • SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration
  • SAP Logistics Management
  • Key Takeaways
  • Looking Ahead
  • Connect with Us

 

What's New in Supply Chain Management

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

The 2608 release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition introduces several enhancements focused on improving supply chain visibility and operational efficiency. New capabilities in inventory management enable better tracking and control of stock levels across multiple locations, while improvements to procurement processes streamline supplier collaboration and purchase order management. The release also includes enhanced analytics capabilities that provide deeper insights into supply chain performance metrics, enabling more informed decision-making. Integration improvements ensure seamless data flow between S/4HANA and other SAP supply chain solutions, creating a more unified technology landscape. These enhancements collectively support organizations in building more resilient and responsive supply chain operations.

🖱Check out more information on the Supply Chain page in the Release Navigator for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition!

 

SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP)

SAP IBP 2608 delivers significant advancements in planning capabilities and user experience. The release introduces enhanced demand sensing algorithms that leverage machine learning to improve forecast accuracy, particularly for products with volatile demand patterns. New features in supply planning enable better constraint-based optimization, allowing planners to model complex manufacturing and distribution scenarios more effectively. The user interface has been refined with improved visualization capabilities, making it easier for planners to identify exceptions and take corrective action quickly. Collaboration features have been strengthened with enhanced workflow capabilities that facilitate cross-functional alignment between sales, operations, and finance teams. Additionally, the release includes performance improvements that enable faster planning cycles, even for organizations managing large-scale, complex supply chain networks.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP IBP page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

SAP Digital Manufacturing

The 2608 release of SAP Digital Manufacturing focuses on enhancing shop floor execution and manufacturing intelligence. New capabilities in production operator dashboards provide real-time visibility into work center performance, quality metrics, and material availability, empowering operators to make informed decisions on the factory floor. Enhanced integration with IoT devices enables better capture of machine data, supporting predictive maintenance initiatives and overall equipment effectiveness (OEE) improvements. The release introduces advanced quality management features that streamline non-conformance handling and corrective action workflows, ensuring faster resolution of quality issues. Manufacturing analytics have been expanded with new pre-built reports and KPIs that provide deeper insights into production performance, downtime analysis, and resource utilization. These enhancements support manufacturers in their journey toward more intelligent, connected, and efficient operations.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP Digital Manufacturing page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

SAP Business Network for Logistics

SAP Business Network for Logistics in the 2608 release brings enhanced visibility and collaboration capabilities for transportation and logistics operations. New features enable better tracking of shipments across multiple carriers and transportation modes, providing end-to-end visibility from origin to destination. The release introduces improved freight collaboration capabilities that streamline communication between shippers, carriers, and logistics service providers, reducing manual coordination efforts and improving on-time delivery performance. Enhanced analytics provide deeper insights into transportation costs, carrier performance, and delivery reliability, enabling data-driven optimization of logistics networks. Integration improvements ensure seamless connectivity with transportation management systems and warehouse management solutions, creating a more unified logistics ecosystem. These capabilities help organizations reduce transportation costs, improve service levels, and build more resilient logistics operations.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP Business Network page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration

The 2608 release of SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration introduces new capabilities designed to improve asset maintenance and service delivery. Enhanced collaboration features enable better coordination between asset owners, service providers, and maintenance teams, ensuring faster response times and more effective issue resolution. The release includes improved work order management capabilities that streamline the entire maintenance lifecycle from planning through execution and closure. New mobile capabilities empower field technicians with access to critical asset information, maintenance history, and technical documentation while on-site, improving first-time fix rates and reducing downtime. Analytics enhancements provide better visibility into asset performance, maintenance costs, and service level achievement, supporting continuous improvement initiatives. These features help organizations maximize asset uptime, extend asset lifecycle's, and optimize maintenance investments.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP Business Network page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

SAP Logistics Management

SAP Logistics Management in the 2608 release delivers enhancements across warehouse and transportation operations. New warehouse management capabilities improve inventory accuracy and picking efficiency through enhanced directed putaway and picking strategies. The release introduces better support for complex warehouse layouts and multi-temperature environments, addressing the needs of organizations with specialized storage requirements. Transportation management enhancements include improved load planning algorithms that optimize vehicle utilization and reduce transportation costs. Enhanced yard management capabilities provide better visibility and control of trailer movements, dock scheduling, and cross-docking operations. Integration improvements ensure seamless coordination between warehouse and transportation processes, enabling more efficient order fulfillment. These enhancements support organizations in building more agile and cost-effective logistics operations.

🖱Check out more information on the SAP Logistics Management page in the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management!

 

Key Takeaways

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition enhances inventory management, procurement processes, and analytics capabilities while improving integration across the supply chain technology landscape.
  • SAP IBP introduces advanced demand sensing, improved supply planning optimization, enhanced user experience, and stronger collaboration features for integrated planning.
  • SAP Digital Manufacturing delivers real-time shop floor visibility, enhanced IoT integration, advanced quality management, and expanded manufacturing analytics for intelligent operations.
  • SAP Business Network for Logistics provides end-to-end shipment visibility, improved freight collaboration, enhanced analytics, and better integration for optimized logistics operations.
  • SAP Business Network Asset Collaboration strengthens maintenance coordination, work order management, mobile capabilities, and asset performance analytics for maximum uptime.
  • SAP Logistics Management improves warehouse efficiency, transportation optimization, yard management, and process integration for agile fulfillment operations.

 

Looking Ahead

The 2608 release represents another significant step forward in SAP's supply chain management portfolio, delivering innovations that address the evolving needs of modern supply chain organizations. From enhanced planning capabilities and manufacturing intelligence to improved collaboration and logistics optimization, these new features provide the tools you need to build more resilient, efficient, and responsive supply chain operations.

We encourage you to explore these new capabilities in detail and consider how they can support your organization's specific supply chain challenges and objectives. As always, the SAP Supply Chain Management community is here to support your journey, share your experiences, ask questions, and learn from peers who are implementing these solutions in their own organizations.

What features in the 2608 release are you most excited about? How are you planning to leverage these new capabilities in your supply chain operations? Share your thoughts and experiences in the comments below!

 

Connect with Us

Your feedback helps shape future Release Navigator content. We encourage you to:

  • Complete our feedback survey
  • Share ideas or questions in the comments
  • Learn more about the Readiness@Scale team
  • Explore additional SAP enablement content

entry.title: Re: Contingent worker in Compensation Module SSF
entry.content:
Hi @Raluca_Barbacariu 

Contigent worker or not it all comes down to one thing for an EC-integrated comp or varpay template to technically allow employees to show on a worksheet if they meet eligibility and they have an active manager : whether they are "EC users" or not. You can check it here in Provisioning.

If they are not EC users and there is a valid reason for it, then you might want to consider a hybrid template.

 

Best,

Xavier

[ERROR] Execute failed: [-141] Table 'foo' not found.

I used print_error routine in the stmt_exec example.

Are you sure that sqlany_execute or sqlany_execute_immediate was the last thing executed?

void
print_error( a_sqlany_connection * sqlany_conn, char * str )
{
    char buffer[SACAPI_ERROR_SIZE];
    int  rc;
    rc = api.sqlany_error( sqlany_conn, buffer, sizeof(buffer));
    printf( "[ERROR] %s: [%d] %s\n", str, rc, buffer );
}
called here for example.                                                          
   ok = api.sqlany_execute( sqlany_stmt );
   if( !ok ) {
       print_error( sqlany_conn, (char *)"Execute failed" );
   }

inside the transaction and commit it.

AND t.table_name = ?), ?, 8, 400, 'N', 'N', 0, 1, 0, 'MS Sans Serif', 8, 400, 'N', 'N', 0, 1, 0, 'MS Sans Serif', 8, 400, 'N', 'N', 0, 1, 0, 'MS Sans Serif', '' );";

break;

}
if( !result )
{
auto i = m_api.sqlany_execute( m_stmt );
if( !m_api.sqlany_execute( m_stmt ) )
{
GetErrorMessage( errorMsg );
result = 1;
break;
}
}
}
}
}

sqlany_execute() returns 0, but calling GetErrorMessage(), which is:

char buffer[SACAPI_ERROR_SIZE + 20] {};
char sqlstate[6];
int rc;
rc = m_api.sqlany_error( m_conn, buffer, SACAPI_ERROR_SIZE + 20 );
m_api.sqlany_sqlstate( m_conn, sqlstate, sizeof( sqlstate ) );
strcat( buffer, ": SQLSTATE: " );
strcat( buffer, sqlstate );
errorMsg.push_back( sqlany_pimpl->m_myconv.from_bytes( buffer ) );
return true;

rc equal 0 and the buffer is empty.

Thank you

entry.title: Quick Fix: System Number Mismatch in MiniSAP (Developer Trial 2022/2023)
entry.content:
When setting up the ABAP Cloud Developer Trial (MiniSAP)—as referenced in the abap-cloud-developer-trial-2022-available-now—you may encounter an issue when installing your trial license via transaction SLICENSE:, transaction SLICENSE fails with this error:

❌ "This system’s system number is “000000000xxxxxx”, but there is a licence key for system number “000000000yyyyy” in the licence key file."

The Cause

The new license generation page: 🔗 https://zlicensekeyminisap-supportportal.dispatcher.hana.ondemand.com/?hc_reset#/minisap

Generates a file named A4H_Multiple.txt that contains a mismatched SYSTEM-NR parameter which does not match your local system.

Quick Solution

  1. Get your System Number: Open SLICENSE in MiniSAP and note your actual System Number (e.g., 000000000xxxxxx).

  2. Edit A4H_Multiple.txt: Open the license file downloaded from the portal in a text editor (Notepad, VS Code, etc.).

  3. Replace SYSTEM-NR: Find the SYSTEM-NR field—which appears twice in the file—and replace its value with your local System Number.

  4. Install: Save the file and upload it in SLICENSE.

Problem solved! 🚀

@SAP

entry.title: Re: SAP RISE - Not able to view EWA report
entry.content:
I reviewed our system and noticed that your S-user was missing the following authorizations as outlined in KBA 2905076 - S-user ID authorizations required for SAP EarlyWatch

  • Service Reports and Feedback
  • Display Security Alerts in SAP EarlyWatch Alert Workspace 
  • Manage Alert(s) in SAP EarlyWatch Alert for all S-users

I have updated your authorizations. You should now be able to view EWA reports.

Could you please check from your side and confirm everything works as expected? If you still run into any issues or need anything else, I’m here to help.

entry.title: Re: How to match Intercompany Stock Transfer , COGS & Rev...
entry.content:
Hi @SagarSurve,

If you want to match document items within same entity (e.g. the sending company of stock transfer), set up a data source with same field as both Leading Unit and Partner Unit and a matching methods with rules that can identify the matching items (such as the ones you proposed G/L accounts and billing type). For more info, see https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/4ebf1502064b406c964b0911adfb3f01/e44f0ad0da5e4cbc81dc6afcc8e135ca.html?ai=true&version=2025.001.
Combining both intercompany sales and intercompany stock transfer P&L into one matching method is fine, as long as the segregation can be done on matching rule level. The reconciliation report is more dependent on Reconciliation Cases/Display Groups.
P&L with BS accounts are not to be mixed up in one data source/matching method, though, as they have different period setup, i.e. cross-year, carryover, etc. See also question 2.2 in https://me.sap.com/notes/2815304.

Let me know if it answers your question. Thanks!

Grace

entry.title: Re: Withholding Tax Not Recognized in SD Customer Invoices
entry.content:
Hi Marlene,

Thank you for your question. Withholding tax in the Sales Process is supported in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, but requires specific configurations to be in place. Please refer to the SAP Help Portal documentation Setting Up Withholding Tax in the Sales Process for detailed guidance.

This topic explains how to configure and check withholding tax in the sales process. Before you begin, you must first have enabled extended withholding tax for your respective company code and defined withholding tax types and codes for each respective country/region for which you are using extended withholding tax. You'll then need to make a series of configuration settings and maintain specific master data as described in the following sections.

Configuring withholding tax for your sales processes involves a series of configuration steps followed by some discrete changes to specific master data. Once these configurations are made and the master data updated, you can run your sales processes normally and verify that the withholding tax is being calculated and posted at various points.

If you have completed all the configuration steps and withholding tax is still not being calculated, please refer to KBA 3383828 - Withholding tax not calculated - SAP for Me - Scenario 12 for further troubleshooting. If the issue persists, please raise a support case in component FI-AP-AP-Q-2CL and our experts will assist you further.

Best regards,
Gordon
SAP Support

entry.title: How to cancel the specific item in the 3PL inbound delivery?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have an interface to a third-party warehouse provider (SAP_COM_0440). A purchase order and the corresponding inbound delivery were transmitted to this warehouse via the interface.

During the goods receipt process, one item was underdelivered, while another item was overdelivered. For the overdelivered item, a separate inbound delivery was created in the purchase order for the excess quantity and then posted. This inbound delivery has been completed successfully. 

As a result, we are now facing the following issue:

  • We cannot complete the purchase order because the inbound delivery has not been fully received.
  • We cannot delete the inbound delivery because this function is not supported by the interface configuration.
  • Since the inbound delivery already exists, we are also unable to modify the purchase order.

Could you please assist us by removing the open line items from the inbound delivery?

We would appreciate your support in resolving this issue.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to cancel the specific item in the 3PL inbound de...
entry.content:
Hi,

Please Note, in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, if the delivery has been distributed to 3PL Warehouse System, the delivery cannot be deleted.

However, you can achieve the equivalent of a deletion operation by performing a zero-out operation to set the delivery item quantity to zero and complete Goods Receipt operation. After completing these operations, the stocks in the delivery are released.

After checking the inbound delivery 180013161 in your system, I noticed you have reversed the Goods Receipt for it. So,

  • If you only want to remove the specific item from the inbound delivery  >>  You can kindly use the SOAP API Warehouse Stock Transfer Receipt Advice – Receive from Warehouse (B2B) to complete Goods Receipt with updating ActualDeliveryQuantity of the specific item to 0. 
  • If you want to cancel the inbound delivery  >>  You can kindly use the SOAP API Warehouse Stock Transfer Receipt Advice – Receive from Warehouse (B2B) to complete Goods Receipt with updating ActualDeliveryQuantity of each item to 0.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP CI Mapping Output - JSON payload output segme...
entry.content: JSON property order mismatch is expected in SAP CPI because JSON objects are inherently unordered. CPI serialization does not preserve schema sequence. If strict property order is required for external API compliance, build the JSON manually using a Groovy script with to enforce insertion order. Otherwise, rely on consumer flexibility since most APIs ignore property order.

entry.title: Re: Material Interchangeability During Goods Receipt
entry.content:
Hello Antonio, 

To fix this layout issue in transaction MM01, run through the troubleshooting steps below: 

 
1. Missing Global MPN Activation
Even if you configured your material type HERB, the Inventory-Managed MPN sub-screen requires a explicit global switch activation in the system: 
  1. Go to transaction SPRO.
  2. Navigate to: Logistics - General  Material Master  Basic Settings  Make Global SETTING 
  3. Ensure that the Manufacturer Part Number (MPN) functionality checkbox is completely selected/active 
2. Missing Sub-Screen Configuration in OMT3
If the field selection group layout does not pull the sub-screen into the Basic Data screen sequence, it will stay hidden:
  1. Run transaction code OMT3 (or go to Material Master 
     
     Configuring the Material Master 
     
     Define Structure of Data Screens for Each Screen Sequence).
     
  2. Select your standard or custom active Screen Sequence (e.g., for Industry standard).
     
  3. Drill down into Data Screens and choose the Basic Data 1 view.
     
  4. Go to the Sub-screens layout table and check if sub-screen SAPLMGMM with screen number 2016 (or the corresponding MPN data container number) is assigned to a sequential block. If it is missing, you must manually add a line entry for this sub-screen profile.
     
 
3. Field Selection Group Hide Rules (OMS9)
If the block is mapped structurally but hidden dynamically via properties, check the field visibility assignment:
 
  1. Run transaction code OMS9.
  2. Find the field selection group associated with fields MARA-MFRPN (Manufacturer Part Number) and MARA-MFRNR (External Manufacturer).
  3. Locate the Field Selection Group assigned to the material type HERB or your active Industry Sector (C - Chemical industry).
  4. Ensure the radio dial indicator is checked for Optional Entry or Display, rather than marked as Hide. 

waiting your kindly feedback 

Regards,

entry.title: Re: Allocation based on planned hours – S/4HANA Cloud Pub...
entry.content:
Follow below steps : 

  1. Define the allocation cycle in the Manage Allocations app
  2. Set the tracing factor: planned hours from WBS
  3. Map cost center → WBS receiver rules,Confirm that activity type configuration supports the tracing factor; note that enabling activity type as a receiver tracing factor may require a configuration entry in table T811FLAGS (field UIWKGI = 'X') if it is not selectable by default
  4. Execute periodic actual cost allocation via Run Allocations app
  5. Analyze results using the Allocation Flow and Allocation Results apps, or reporting apps such as Project Financial Booklet – Professional Services or Monitor Projects

Useful SAP Learning journey - https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/performing-overhead-cost-controlling

entry.title: @sap/cds was loaded from different locations
entry.content:
Environment

  • npm: 11.12.1
  • Node.js: 24.13.1
  • @SAP/cds-dk (global): 10.0.5

Hello,

I'm running into a very strange issue with a newly created CAP project, and I'm hoping someone can shed some light on what's happening.

The project is basically a bare CAP application. If it only contains the backend service, everything works fine. However, as soon as I add a generated Fiori application, the server fails to start with the following error:

$ npm start

> nai-rtr-budget-mgmt@1.0.0 start
> cds serve

[cds-plugin-ui5] [INFO] Running cds-plugin-ui5@0.13.6 (@sap/cds-dk@9.9.3, @SAP/cds@9.9.3)
[cds-plugin-ui5] [INFO] Mounting /budgetadmin to UI5 app C:\Dev\nai-rtr-budget-mgmt\app\budget-admin (id=budget-admin)
[cds] [INFO] loaded model from 5 file(s):
 
  srv\index.cds
  app\services.cds
  app\budget-admin\annotations.cds
  srv\budget-service.cds
  db\budget.cds

[cds] [INFO] connect to db > sqlite { url: 'db.sqlite' }
[cds] [INFO] using auth strategy {
  kind: 'mocked',
  impl: 'node_modules\\@sap\\cds\\lib\\srv\\middlewares\\auth\\basic-auth.js'
}
[cds] [INFO] serving BudgetService {
  at: [ '/odata/v4/budget' ],
  decl: 'srv\\budget-service.cds:3',
  impl: 'node_modules\\@sap\\cds\\srv\\app-service.js'
}
info fiori-tools-proxy Using UI5 version 1.150.0 based on manifest.json
info fiori-tools-proxy Starting fiori-tools-proxy using following configuration:
info fiori-tools-proxy proxy: 'undefined'
info fiori-tools-proxy ignoreCertErrors: 'false'
info fiori-tools-proxy backend: undefined
info fiori-tools-proxy ui5: [{"path":"/resources","url":"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com","version":"1.150.0"},{"path":"/test-resources","url":"https://sapui5.hana.ondemand.com","version":"1.150.0"}]
info fiori-tools-proxy debug: 'false'
info fiori-tools-appreload Livereload middleware started for port 35729 and path C:\Dev\nai-rtr-budget-mgmt\app\budget-admin\webapp
(node:29036) [DEP0190] DeprecationWarning: Passing args to a child process with shell option true can lead to security vulnerabilities, as the arguments are not escaped, only concatenated.
(Use `node --trace-deprecation ...` to show where the warning was created)
[cds] [INFO] loaded model from 5 file(s):
 
  srv\index.cds
  app\services.cds
  app\budget-admin\annotations.cds
  srv\budget-service.cds
  db\budget.cds

[cds] [INFO] loaded model from 5 file(s):
 
  srv\index.cds
  app\services.cds
  app\budget-admin\annotations.cds
  srv\budget-service.cds
  db\budget.cds

info fiori-tools-preview Initialized for app budgetadmin
[cds-plugin-ui5] [ERROR] Failed to inject application pages to welcome page! The links of the application pages may not work properly!

-----------------------------------------------------------------------
ERROR: @SAP/cds was loaded from different locations:
 
  c:\dev\nai-rtr-budget-mgmt\node_modules\@sap\cds
  ~\appdata\roaming\npm\node_modules\@sap\cds-dk\node_modules\@sap\cds 

Ensure a single install to avoid hard-to-resolve errors.
-----------------------------------------------------------------------

I can work around the issue by uninstalling the globally installed @SAP/cds-dk, but that doesn't seem like a real solution.

What's confusing is that I have another CAP project on the same machine that also uses cds-plugin-ui5, and it works perfectly regardless of whether @SAP/cds-dk is installed globally or not. I also noticed that project even has @SAP/cds-dk installed locally as a dev dependency, yet it still runs without any issues.

Here are the things I've already tried:

  • Matched the package versions between the working and non-working projects.
  • Downgraded the globally installed @SAP/cds-dk from v10 to v9 (since the working project was originally created with v9).
  • Installed @SAP/cds-dk locally instead of relying on the global installation.
  • Verified that the issue only occurs after adding the Fiori application. A backend-only CAP project runs fine.

None of the above made any difference. The new project continued to fail while the existing project continued to work.

The only things I've found that make the new project work are:

  1. Completely removing any additional @SAP/cds-dk installation so only a single installation is available.

Strangely, adding a .env file containing: DEBUG=all also allows the project to start, even with the global @SAP/cds-dk installed. I don't understand why enabling debug logging would affect this behavior.

Has anyone encountered something similar before? More importantly, why would two CAP projects on the same machine behave differently when they appear to have the same tooling and dependency versions? Is there anything specific I should compare between the two projects to determine why one resolves @SAP/cds correctly while the other does not?

Any suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: Error AFABN
entry.content:
Solution Checklist

1. Verify AO90 Account Determination: - Check all G/L accounts maintained for Depreciation Area 08. Ensure no mandatory account is missing (Asset Balance Sheet, Accumulated Depreciation, Depreciation Expense

2. Check Reconciliation Account Settings: - If Area 08 is an inflation/posted periodically or depreciation-only area, the assigned G/L accounts must not be Fixed Asset Reconciliation Accounts. As per SAP guidance (Note 7595), reconciliation accounts are generally only appropriate for the main area posted in real time (typically Area)

3. Verify Depreciation Area Customizing (OADB/OAYZ): - Confirm Area 08 is correctly defined as an inflation area. Check whether the posting indicator (real-time/periodic/not posted) is configured correctly.

4. Check Account Assignment

  1. Ensure all required accounts exist for
  2. Company Code G235
  3. Chart of Accounts
  4. Asset Class
  5. Account Determination

5. Run Consistency Checks

  1. Execute AW01N and verify APC values and planned depreciation.
  2. Check ANLB/ANLC data consistency if required.

6. Review SAP Notes

  1. 3490950 – AAPO191 "Asset row is not complete.
  2. 2073134 – Errors AAPO510/AAPO191 during AFAB
  3. 859617 – AAPO191 in depreciation posting (referenced by SAP Communi

Since you have already verified:

  1.  AO90 configuration
  2. Asset Master (AS03)
  3. Asset Values (AW01N)
  4. Planned depreciation
  5. SAP Notes

The next thing to verify is whether the G/L accounts assigned to Depreciation Area 08 are incorrectly marked as Asset Reconciliation Accounts in FS00. This is the most common root cause of AAPO191 for inflation or periodically posted depreciation areas.

If you can share: S/4HANA or ECC version

  1. Screenshot of AO90 for Area 08
  2. Full AAPO191 error log (SLG1/AFABN log)

I can help identify the exact configuration issue.

entry.title: Re: Issue in order settlement thorugh KO88
entry.content:
The settlement through KO88 cannot be executed because it is linked to an asset in FY2025, which is already closed.

To complete the settlement, FY2025 must be temporarily reopened, the settlement executed, and then the fiscal year closed again.

entry.title: Re: No item category exists (Table T184L ZUMB NORM CHSP)...
entry.content: Hi @Beto1: Whenever batch determination takes place, the system uses the combination of Delivery Type (ZUMB) + Item Category Group (NORM) + Item Usage (CHSP). Please verify whether the batch-related settings have been maintained. If they are maintained, the system will determine Item Usage = CHSP automatically. For this same combination (ZUMB + NORM + CHSP), you also need to maintain the corresponding Item Category Determination settings so that the correct item category is determined during delivery.

entry.title: Re: @sap/cds was loaded from different locations
entry.content:
I faced exactly the same problem yesterday; I had a quick look at it and managed to solve it using the following variable: CDS_PLUGIN_UI5_ACTIVE=false

It’s not the best solution, because you always have to rebuild the apps, but it works.

entry.title: Re: How can we bring all three currencies — LC, FC, and G...
entry.content:
Hi Aagya,

FC means Functional currency, right?

in ACDOCU there are 3 currency key figures. TC-Transaction Currency, LC-Local Currency and GC-Group Currency.

TC will be transferred from ACDOCA, when the FS item has a breakdown by TC in its BDC.

If you fill target key figure GC from e.g.  global currency source key figure, then you cannot transfer both currencies , LC and FC in parallel because you have only LC as target key figure.

BR

entry.title: How to directly add returnable packaging items in Outbound Delivery without VL316 error?
entry.content:
Hi experts,
We are working with Scope Item BDW (Returnable Packaging Processing) and facing an issue when adding a returnable packaging material directly as a line item in an Outbound Delivery (VL02N / Manage Outbound Deliveries).

[Issue]
When adding the item directly to the Outbound Delivery, we get the following error:
Error Message: VL316 - Sales area xxxx 00 00 is not permitted.
Context: The material master uses a common distribution channel (00). When added directly in the delivery, the system tries to determine the sales area using channel 00, which is not set up for this sales org/plant.

[Observed Behavior]
If we add the returnable packaging item in the Sales Order (SO), it works fine because the line item inherits the header distribution channel (e.g., 10).

[Question]
Is there any workaround to allow adding line items directly in the Outbound Delivery using the header distribution channel (or bypassing the common channel 00 determination)?
Any insights or recommendations on how to handle this requirement directly within the delivery document would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere: Understanding TCUR* Tables and Practical Implementation
entry.content:
Hi

Congratulations with the blog. The topic has been touched by many others and I don't see anything new, sorry. If you want to use AI, fine but use it to do something new and not seen before.

You write "Financial reports", it is my experience that it is very difficult to mimic the currency conversion happening in financial reports and you should in general avoid it. The problem most often comes down to being able to find the correct reference date, it is for sure not the posting date but rather the "Exchange Rate Date". In finance the currency conversion is already handled using more measures in different currencies.

with kind regards

Kristian

entry.title: SAP MDK FioriToolbar OnPress events are triggered for the wrong button
entry.content:
I'm facing an issue with the SAP MDK FioriToolbar using the latest MDK version.

Scenario

I have a page with three FioriToolbar buttons:

  • Confirm
  • Next
  • Previous

    Each button has its own OnPress rule.

    ConfirmItem

    • Title: Confirm
    • OnPress: $(PLT,'/Actions/NavigateToConfirm.action','/Actions/NavigateToConfirm.action','/Rules/NavigateToConfirmWeb.js')

      NextItem

      • Title: Next
      • OnPress: /Rules/CreatePONextButton.js

        PreviousItem

        • Title: Previous

OnPress: /Rules/CreatePOPreviousButton.js

Issue

When I click the Previous button, the Confirm button's OnPress rule (NavigateToConfirmWeb.js) is also executed.

I verified this by adding console.log() statements to each rule.

The output is:

CreatePOPreviousButton
NavigateToConfirmWeb

The Previous rule does not call NavigateToConfirmWeb, so it appears that the framework is triggering the Confirm button's OnPress handler.

 

Additional observations

If I remove the Confirm button from the toolbar, the Previous button works correctly.

The issue occurs only when all three toolbar buttons are present.

The Previous rule does not navigate to the Confirm page.

The Confirm button is the only one using a $(PLT,...) expression, while the other buttons directly reference JavaScript rules.

I am testing using the latest MDK Web client.

Has anyone experienced this issue before?

Is this a known issue with FioriToolbar on the MDK Web client?

Is mixing $(PLT,...) and JavaScript rules in the same toolbar supported?

Are there any recommended workarounds?

Thanks in advance for any suggestions

entry.title: Re: IAS for ONB2.0 New Hires – 3 (Transformations)
entry.content:
Hi @jaideepshetty and All, 

Thanks for the detailed blogs on this topic. 

We have one query regarding IAS Email template locale. 

The IAS consider default locale of the new hire to send IAS email template in that particular locale (provided template in that locale is maintained in IAS). 

If we initiate onboarding manually using Add new hire to Onboarding with locale field given the mail is sent in that locale.

However we have observed that the default locale for onboardees initiated from "RCM" is by default set to en_US (Default language is set as en_US in our system). So, IAS welcome email is sent in English (United States). All of our candidates are going through RCM. However we wish to send IAS welcome email in different locale based on country/region of job info of new hire.

What logic options do we have in IAS to achieve this?

Has anyone found out solution for this.

Thanks.
